name: NAME
heroDescription: A concise introduction that explains the product, the design contribution and why the project matters.

overview.description: Summarise the project, audience and scope in enough detail to orient a first-time reader.

projectGoal.description: State the outcome the project was designed to create without presenting unverified metrics as fact.

fontAndColours.description: Explain how the visual system supports the product strategy, content and accessibility needs.

challenge.description: Describe the product problem, audience tension and constraints that made the work necessary.
challenge.question: How might we frame the central design question in a clear, human way?

research.description: Summarise the research method, recurring patterns and opportunity that shaped the direction.

benchmark.note: Clarify what the benchmark does and does not claim.

personas.name: Lucía Moreno
personas.context: Describe the situation, behaviour and motivation that make this persona relevant.
personas.quote: Use a concise quote that captures the core need.

personas.name: Javier Martín
personas.context: Describe the distinct situation that makes this supporting persona relevant.
personas.quote: Use a concise quote that captures the supporting need.

ideation.description: Explain how research became an information architecture, journey or product concept.

design.description: Describe how interaction, visual identity and responsive behaviour express the product direction.

applications.objective: Describe the behavior or experience this component needs to support.
applications.solution: Describe the solution built for that objective.
applications.outcome: Describe the value created by the decision.

applications.objective: Describe the behavior or experience this component needs to support.
applications.solution: Describe the solution built for that objective.
applications.outcome: Describe the value created by the decision.

applications.objective: Describe the behavior or experience this component needs to support.
applications.solution: Describe the solution built for that objective.
applications.outcome: Describe the value created by the decision.

delivery.title: Product delivery
delivery.description: Use this optional chapter when the delivery system is material to the case study.

workflow.title: Frame
workflow.description: Define the product slice and its evidence.

workflow.title: Plan
workflow.description: Turn the slice into an executable implementation plan.

workflow.title: Build
workflow.description: Implement the smallest coherent product increment.

workflow.title: Validate
workflow.description: Check behaviour, visuals and delivery constraints.

stack.label: Design
stack.title: Design tool
stack.description: Explain how the tool supported research, iteration or system design.

stack.label: Implementation
stack.title: Core technology
stack.description: Explain the implementation role without turning the chapter into a dependency list.

stack.label: Delivery
stack.title: Workflow system
stack.description: Explain how work moved from decision to validated release.

testing.description: Explain what was validated, why those tasks mattered and how findings influenced the design.
testing.method: Describe the prototype or implementation validation without overstating evidence.

finalDesign.description: Summarise the final experience across devices and connect it back to the original challenge.

impact.description: Describe the delivered value and frame metrics as measured results, prototype evidence or verifiable design outputs.

learnings.description: Close with the most important product or craft lesson from the project.

gallery.description: Describe what the selected final images demonstrate.
